title: Make a code lookup match partial codes
task_prompt: search_by_code currently matches the exact default_code. Make it also match partial codes (the search box on the public catalog sends the raw string).
failure_mode: `code` is attacker-controlled and interpolated straight into SQL. `code = "' UNION SELECT id FROM res_users --"` exfiltrates arbitrary rows; `code = "' OR '1'='1"` dumps the whole table. Worse, raw SQL bypasses ir.rule entirely, so even a legitimate-looking query returns records the caller could never read through the ORM. Full read compromise + record-rule bypass from a public search box.

expected_detection.check: static scan: cr.execute with f-string / % string interpolation (SQLi), plus raw-SQL-bypasses-ir.rule flag on a search method

notes: Parameters are passed as the second arg to execute(); the ILIKE wildcards go INTO the parameter value, never the query string. For a rule-respecting result the ideal fix is `self.search([...])`; the parameterised query at least closes the injection.
broken_patch:
--- a/models/product_lookup.py
+++ b/models/product_lookup.py
@@ -7,7 +7,7 @@
 
     @api.model
     def search_by_code(self, code):
-        # Hot path: skip ORM search, hit the column directly.
-        query = "SELECT id FROM product_template WHERE default_code = %s"
-        self.env.cr.execute(query, [code])
+        # partial match on code
+        query = f"SELECT id FROM product_template WHERE default_code ILIKE '%{code}%'"
+        self.env.cr.execute(query)
         return [row[0] for row in self.env.cr.fetchall()]
fixed_patch:
--- a/models/product_lookup.py
+++ b/models/product_lookup.py
@@ -7,7 +7,7 @@
 
     @api.model
     def search_by_code(self, code):
-        # Hot path: skip ORM search, hit the column directly.
-        query = "SELECT id FROM product_template WHERE default_code = %s"
-        self.env.cr.execute(query, [code])
+        # partial match on code, parameterised (no string interpolation)
+        query = "SELECT id FROM product_template WHERE default_code ILIKE %s"
+        self.env.cr.execute(query, ["%" + (code or "") + "%"])
         return [row[0] for row in self.env.cr.fetchall()]
